message: I've been digging into home fire prep lately, and honestly, it's less about catastrophizing and more about having a solid plan that actually fits your life. Found a really helpful resource from Ready.gov that breaks down the practical stuff—escape routes, smoke alarms, what to grab on the way out—in a way that doesn't feel overwhelming. Turns out a little planning beforehand means way less stress if something actually happens. What's one fire-safety thing you've already got handled at your place?
